oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Perspectives on The Future of Pionus Parrots in American Aviculture (part 1 of 2)
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Sargent, Mark
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Much has been written about Pionus parrots in the USA since the 1980's and information is widely available on this genus from books, articles, and information on the internet regarding geographic distribution in the wild, care, husbandry, diets, breeding, and suitability as pets and companion animals. Because these sources are readily available, and are not the focus of this paper, the author only briefly touches on these topics as needed as they relate to the points covered in this article. Opinions are of the author and not necessarily the view of the AFA. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): Volume 52 July 2025 issue of AFA Watchbird
